schedule:
Okay, let’s get you back on track and build a robust recovery and flexibility plan, considering your recent soccer injuries. As a registered sports dietitian and personal trainer, my priority is to safely rebuild your strength and mobility while minimizing further strain. This schedule focuses on controlled movements, prioritizing healing and preventing re-injury, alongside a tailored nutrition plan to support your recovery. We’ll be incorporating mobility work, targeted strength, and mindful stretching – let’s build a solid foundation for your fitness journey. 

**Important Disclaimer:** *Before starting any new exercise program, consult with your physician or physical therapist to ensure it’s appropriate for your specific injuries and limitations. Listen to your body and stop if you feel any sharp pain.*


---

**Monday: Dynamic Mobility & Core Stability**

*   **Warm-up (10 mins):**
    *   Arm Circles (Forward & Backward): 30 seconds each – Initiate with small circles, gradually increasing the size.
    *   Leg Swings (Forward & Sideways): 10 reps each leg – Maintain a slight bend in the knee and control the movement.
    *   Torso Twists: 30 seconds – Keep your core engaged and rotate from your waist.
*   **Main (40 mins):**
    *   Plank (3 sets, 60-90 seconds hold): Maintain a straight line from head to heels, engaging your core.
    *   Bird Dog (3 sets, 10 reps per side): Extend one arm and the opposite leg simultaneously, keeping your back flat.
    *   Glute Bridges (3 sets, 15 reps): Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement, ensuring your knees remain aligned with your hips.
    *   Cat-Cow Stretch (3 sets, 1 minute): Move between arching your back (cat) and dropping your belly (cow), coordinating with your breath.
*   **Cool-down (10 mins):**
    *   Child’s Pose: Hold for 60 seconds – Rest your forehead on the mat and extend your arms forward.
    *   Hamstring Stretch (Standing or Lying): Hold for 30 seconds per leg – Keep your back straight and gently reach towards your toes.
*   **Rest Day Recommendation:** Active recovery – light walking or gentle yoga.
*   **Recovery Tip:** Epsom salt bath – soak for 20 minutes to reduce inflammation and muscle soreness.


**Tuesday: Upper Body & Shoulder Mobility**

*   **Warm-up (10 mins):**
    *   Shoulder Rolls (Forward & Backward): 30 seconds each – Focus on full range of motion, feeling the muscles rotate.
    *   Wrist Circles: 30 seconds – Rotate your wrists in both directions to improve flexibility.
    *   Dynamic Chest Stretch (Arm across body): 10 reps per side – Gently pull your arm across your body, feeling a stretch in your chest.
*   **Main (40 mins):**
    *   Push-Ups (3 sets, as many reps as possible): Maintain a straight line from head to heels, lowering your chest towards the floor.
    *   Dumbbell Rows (3 sets, 10 reps per arm): Pull the dumbbell towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
    *   Overhead Press (3 sets, 8 reps): Press the dumbbells overhead, keeping your core engaged.
    *   Wall Slides (3 sets, 10 reps): Slide your arms up a wall, maintaining a 90-degree angle at your elbows.
*   **Cool-down (10 mins):**
    *   Doorway Pec Stretch: Hold for 60 seconds – Place your forearms on a doorway frame and lean forward, feeling a stretch in your chest.
    *   Triceps Stretch (Overhead): Hold for 30 seconds per arm – Reach one arm overhead and bend at the elbow, gently pulling your elbow towards your head.
*   **Rest Day Recommendation:** Foam rolling – focus on legs and back.
*   **Recovery Tip:** Hydrate consistently throughout the day – aim for at least 8 glasses of water.



**Wednesday: Lower Body & Hip Mobility**

*   **Warm-up (10 mins):**
    *   Hip Circles: 30 seconds each direction – Rotate your hips in a circular motion, maintaining a stable core.
    *   Leg Swings (Forward & Sideways): 10 reps each leg – Maintain a slight bend in the knee and control the movement.
    *   Ankle Rolls: 30 seconds each direction – Rotate your ankles in both directions to improve mobility.
*   **Main (40 mins):**
    *   Bodyweight Squats (3 sets, 12 reps): Maintain a straight back and descend as if sitting in a chair.
    *   Lunges (3 sets, 10 reps per leg): Step forward with one leg and lower your body until both knees are bent at 90 degrees.
    *   Romanian Deadlifts (3 sets, 10 reps): Hinge at your hips, keeping your back straight and lowering the dumbbells towards the floor.
    *   Pigeon Pose (3 sets, 30 seconds per side): Gently stretch your hips and glutes by bringing one leg forward and resting it on your hip.
*   **Cool-down (10 mins):**
    *   Butterfly Stretch: Hold for 60 seconds – Bring the soles of your feet together and gently press your knees towards the floor.
    *   Hip Flexor Stretch (Kneeling): Hold for 30 seconds per side – Gently push your hips forward, feeling a stretch in the front of your hips.
*   **Rest Day Recommendation:** Gentle stretching and light cardio (cycling).
*   **Recovery Tip:** Prioritize sleep – aim for 7-9 hours of quality sleep.



**Thursday: Flexibility & Active Recovery**

*   **Warm-up (10 mins):**
    *   Dynamic Stretching: Focus on movements that mimic soccer – high knees, butt kicks, carioca.
    *   Torso Twists: 30 seconds – Keep your core engaged and rotate from your waist.
*   **Main (40 mins):**
    *   Yoga Flow (30-45 minutes): Focus on poses that improve flexibility and balance – downward dog, warrior poses, triangle pose.
    *   Foam Rolling (10 mins): Target major muscle groups – quads, hamstrings, calves, back.
*   **Cool-down (10 mins):**
    *   Supine Spinal Twist: Hold for 60 seconds per side – Gently twist your spine, supporting yourself with your hands.
    *   Reclining Pigeon Pose: Hold for 60 seconds per side – Relax into a comfortable stretch, supporting your hips with pillows.
*   **Rest Day Recommendation:** Complete rest – allow your body to fully recover.
*   **Recovery Tip:**  Consume a protein-rich snack (Greek yogurt with berries) to aid muscle repair.



---

**Daily Meal Plan (Example - Adjust portions to your individual needs):**

**Pre-Workout (30-60 mins before):**
*   Oatmeal (1/2 cup dry) with 1 scoop protein powder (whey or plant-based) and 1/2 cup berries (Macros: 350 calories, 30g protein, 40g carbs, 10g fat)

**Post-Workout (Within 30-60 mins):**
*   Chicken Breast (4oz) with Sweet Potato (1 medium) and Steamed Broccoli (1 cup) (Macros: 450 calories, 40g protein, 50g carbs, 15g fat)

**Breakfast (7:00 AM):** Greek Yogurt (1 cup) with Granola (1/4 cup) and Banana (1 medium) (Macros: 300 calories, 25g protein, 40g carbs, 8g fat)
**Mid-Morning Snack (10:00 AM):** Apple with 2 tablespoons Almond Butter (Macros: 200 calories, 7g protein, 25g carbs, 10g fat)
**Lunch (1:00 PM):** Turkey Breast Sandwich on Whole Wheat Bread with Lettuce, Tomato, and Avocado (Macros: 400 calories, 30g protein, 45g carbs, 15g fat)
**Afternoon Snack (4:00 PM):** Protein Shake (1 scoop protein powder with water or almond milk) (Macros: 150 calories, 25g protein, 5g carbs, 2g fat)
**Dinner (7:00 PM):** Salmon (4oz) with Quinoa (1/2 cup cooked) and Asparagus (1 cup) (Macros: 500 calories, 35g protein, 50g carbs, 20g fat)

Remember to adjust this plan based on your individual caloric needs and preferences.  Consistent hydration throughout the day is crucial.

Let me know if you’d like me to refine any aspect of this plan or delve deeper into specific exercises or nutritional strategies!
